rulings.Case Title: KRISTEN ECKERT VS. BRADLEY & COMPANY, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2019, Line 1 DEFENDANT BRADLEY & COMPANY, LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS This motion is moot in light of the Court's ruling granting terminating sanctions. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: KRISTEN ECKERT VS. BRADLEY & COMPANY, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanction, Or In The Alternative Evidentiary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT BRADLEY & COMPANY, LLC,'S Motion For Terminating Sanction, Or In The Alternative Evidentiary Sanction Defendants Michael D. Bradley and Bradley & Company, LLC's motion for terminating sanctions is granted, the Second Amended Complaint is ordered stricken, and Plaintiff is ordered to pay Defendants $4,085.43 for the reasonable expenses, including attorney's fees, incurred by Defendants as a result of her conduct. (Code Civ. Proc. §§ 2023.030(d), 2025.450(h).) Plaintiff Kristen Eckert failed to appear at her properly noticed deposition or to serve responses to Defendants' interrogatories. Despite attempts by Defendants' counsel to obtain a new deposition date, Plaintiff's attorney never offered an alternative date, and did not respond to attempts to confer regarding her interrogatory responses. The Court granted Defendants' unopposed motion to compel Plaintiff to appear for her deposition, to respond to interrogatories, and to pay sanctions. Despite the Court's order, Plaintiff did not serve responses to Defendants' interrogatories, and she failed to obey the Court's order that she appear for her deposition on the date ordered by the Court. She also failed to respond to requests for admissions served by Defendants, and the Court granted Defendants' unopposed motion to deem those requests admitted. Finally, she did not file any opposition to the instant motion. As Defendants aptly observe, Plaintiff has abandoned her own case. Plaintiff's repeated failures to provide timely discovery responses and to comply with the Court's orders constitute willful, repeated, and egregious misuse of the discovery process that warrants the imposition of terminating sanctions. (See, e.g., CREED-21 v. City of Wildomar (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 690, 701-703; Department of Forestry and Fire Protection v. Howell (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 154, 197-198; Los Defensores, Inc. v. Gomez (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 377, 390-392.) No less severe sanction would be effective to produce full compliance with the discovery rules or to ensure Defendants receive a fair trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT LEE VS. ERNEST LOPEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant To Answer Form Interrogatories Special Interrogatories Requests For Production Of Documents And Produce Documents Without Objections And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2019, Line 6 - PLAINTIFF ALBERT LEE's Motion To Compel Defendant To Answer Form Interrogatories Special Interrogatories Requests For Production Of Documents And Produce Documents Without Objections And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. JPT Highman will contact the parties with a substantive tentative ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, then the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing if the tentative ruling is contested. If the tentative is not contested and there is no hearing on the motion, then Defendant must timely comply with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1312. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EVA ARMADA ET AL VS. MICHAEL GUMBAN PANGILINAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion To Further Compel Defendants' Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions And Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2019, Line 1 - PLAINTIFF EVA ARMADA, ANTONIO SALSEDO, RUDY CONCEPCION and LORNA BINALEY's Motion To Further Compel Defendants' Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions And Attorney'S Fees. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted as to form interrogatories 2.1, 2.2, 12.1, and 15.1 with verified amended answers being due by personal service on July 19. Denied as to 17. Defendants don't oppose as to 2.1 and 2.2. As to 12.1 and 15.1, CCP requires the motion to be served within 45 days of the verified response. It is true that no verification was required for the responses to 12.1 and 15.1 since they were just objections. But the CCP sets no deadline to move to compel as to unverified responses. Plaintiff's failure to meet and confer with defendants about scheduling the date of hearing of the motion in violation of the local rules, although improper, is not grounds for denying the motion. Defendants apparently don't have a problem with the date since there is no evidence they sought to change it. Whether or not the lawsuit is technically proper is not an issue for this motion. The court set a trial date in August, so discovery must proceed until the discovery cutoff, or the case is dismissed, whichever is first. The motion is denied as to interrogatory 17. The "Yes" answer is proper unless defendants did not admit any of the requests for admission. There is no evidence in the papers that defendants did not admit any of the requests for admission. Sanctions of $1,500 are awarded. The failure to answer 2.1 and 2.2, and the objections to 12.1 and 15.1 are without justification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, then the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing if the tentative ruling is contested. If the tentative is not contested and there is no hearing on the motion, then Defendant must timely comply with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1312. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROMULA VILLANUEVA VS. SANTOS A. CADIENTE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT SONNY CADIENTE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION/Summary Judgment Defendant Sonny Agonoy Cadiente's motion for summary judgment is denied. The claims against the Estate of Decedent Mary B. Agonoy arose at the time of the Decedent's death, not in the conservatorship matter (Case No. PCN-15-299338). Therefore, the representations Plaintiff Romula Villanueva made that she was not a creditor of the estate in the Petitions for conservatorship filed prior to Decedent's death are consistent with Plaintiff's current position as a creditor. (SUMF Nos. 2-3.) As such, there is no basis for judicial or collateral estoppel. Moreover, the Probate Code requires a claim to be filed with the Estate before a separate lawsuit can be instituted. (Probate Code §§ 9000, 9002, 9256; Wilkison v. Wiederkehr (2002) 101 Cal.App.4th 822, 828-831.) Plaintiff complied with this procedure and her allegations in the first amended complaint are consistent with her creditor's claim. Res judicata is also inapplicable as Defendant fails to identify a final judgment on the merits of Plaintiff's status as a creditor of the estate. Plaintiff raises a triable dispute of fact over the adequacy of compensation afforded to her. Plaintiff states that the compensation provided to her was less than the value of the services rendered and that she sacrificed her old job to take care of Decedent. (Villanueva Decl. 2.) Although Plaintiff received an annuity, reimbursement of costs for the conservatorship, and compensation for her caretaker services, there remains a dispute of fact as to whether Plaintiff was fully compensated and whether there was adequate consideration for the promise that was allegedly made to her by Decedent. (UMF Nos. 15-21; AUMF Nos. 1-3; see Byrne v. Laura (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 1054, 1063-1066 [summary adjudication is an inappropriate means of resolving ambiguities regarding contract for support of decedent]; Gomes v. Borba (1950) 99 Cal.App.2d 38, 42 [adequacy of consideration is a matter for the trier of fact].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. TURO INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Turo Inc.'S Re-Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs And Cross-Defendants Further Responses To Turos First And Second Sets Of Requests For Production.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2019, Line 3 - DEFENDANT TURO INC.'s Re-Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs And Cross-Defendants Further Responses To Turos First And Second Sets Of Requests For Production. (Part 2 of 2) Requests 22, 23, 34, 35, 39- Granted in part. Communications with agents of rental car companies acting on behalf of the rental car companies should be produced. The court doesn't see why a distinction should be made between communications with rental car agencies and communications with agents of rental car companies acting on behalf of the rental car company. In addition to the agencies it says it will search, the City is ordered to search for responsive documents with the Controller. Denied as to other City agencies. Request 28- See Requests 22, et al., and 10, et al., above. Request 32- The Request just requests documents in the custody of the Clerk of the Board of Supervisors. Therefore, Turo's request that the City for documents in the custody of other agencies is denied. Communications with agents of rental car companies acting on behalf of the rental car companies should be produced. The court doesn't see why a distinction should be made between communications with rental car agencies and communications with agents of rental car companies acting on behalf of the rental car company. Request 33- Hearing required. The City says it agrees to produce responsive documents with any Enterprise entity identified by Turo. Does Turo have a problem with that? What are the Enterprise entities identified by Turo? Communications with agents of Enterprise acting on behalf of Enterprise should be produced. The court doesn't see why a distinction should be made between communications with Enterprise and communications with agents of Enterprise acting on behalf of Enterprise. In addition to the agencies it says it will search, the City is ordered to search for responsive documents with the Controller. Denied as to other City agencies. Requests 40, 41- Hearing required. See Requests 1, et al., and 22, et al., above. Request 42- Denied. Relevance appears peripheral. Too burdensome to redact massive amounts of private information. Request 46- Granted in part. Hearing required. See Request 2, et al., above. Do the agreements with AAAE contain any private information. It would generally seem that a government agency's contracts are not private. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, then the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing if the tentative ruling is contested. If the tentative is not contested and there is no hearing on the motion, then Defendant must timely comply with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1312. Tentative ruling part 2 of 2 =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. TURO INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Previously Filed Motion And Consolidated Motion To Compel Plaintiffs And Cross-Defendants Further Responses To Turos First And Second Sets Of Requests For Production
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2019, Line 3 - DEFENDANT TURO INC.'s Re-Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs And Cross-Defendants Further Responses To Turos First And Second Sets Of Requests For Production. (Part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Requests 1, 4, 5, 30, 31- Hearing required. Granted in part. In addition to the agencies it says it will search, the City is ordered to search for responsive documents with the Controller. Denied as to other City agencies. As to the pre-2011 records, on other requests Turo said it would be satisfied with formal reports and analyses regarding ground transportation burdens beginning from the date when SFO first implemented each ground transportation charge. On these requests, Turo did not say that. Was that an oversight, or does Turo want all responsive non-privileged pre-2011 documents on these requests? Requests 2, 3, 6-9, 12, 16, 20, 21, 24, 36-38, 43-45, 47- Granted in part. In addition to the agencies it says it will search, the City is ordered to search for responsive documents with the Controller. Denied as to other City agencies. Requests 10, 11, 13-15, 25-27, 29. Hearing required. Granted in part. The City will produce responsive documents from 2011 through the present from the agencies it lists plus the Controller. Denied as to other agencies. The court doesn't presently understand why the production of the pre-2011 documents in Turo's compromise proposal would be burdensome- formal reports and analyses regarding ground transportation burdens beginning from the date when SFO first implemented each ground transportation charge-- but wants the People to explain why it would be if it would be. It would appear that this is a request for specified documents that would not appear to be privileged. The court is tentatively inclined to allow Turo to take a PMK deposition regarding the City's burdensome objection to find out what search terms were used and to obtain other pertinent information and then hold another hearing. Requests 17-19. See tentative as to Requests 10, 11, et al., above. Also hearing required re production of "incomplete collections of data" or draft reports or studies. The court doesn't know if it would be unduly burdensome to produce draft reports or studies. It would seem tentatively that these are specified documents that would be attached to emails. The court doesn't quite know what "incomplete collections of data" covers. Tentative ruling Part 1 of 2 =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. TURO INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Re-Notice) Of Previously Filed Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs And Cross-Defendants Further Responses To Second Set Of Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2019, Line 3 - DEFENDANT TURO INC.'s Re-Notice of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs And Cross-Defendants Further Responses To Second Set Of Special Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Interrogatory 10. Granted. Until its legal theory is rejected, Turo has a right to ask interrogatories based on its legal theory of the case, whether or not its theory turns out to be correct. It is beyond the scope of this motion to determine if its theory is correct, a determination which perhaps can't be made until the discovery is completed. The People have not provided a responsive answer. They have to respond as to the benefits for Turo or any peer-to-peer car sharing program and how the charges bear a rational relationship to the benefits conferred on Turo or any peer-to-peer car sharing program. Interrogatory 11. Hearing required. The court isn't sure what the agreement between the parties was regarding the amended response and wants to discuss that with the parties. Also, the court wants to know when the people can provide the amended answers. Interrogatory 12. Hearing required as to what information the People are going to provide in their amended response and when they will provide it. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, then the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing if the tentative ruling is contested. If the tentative is not contested and there is no hearing on the motion, then Defendant must timely comply with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1312. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KADEN WOODALL ET AL VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANTS REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, PAUL STEWART, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants Regents of University of California and Paul Stewart, M.D.'s demurrer to Plaintiffs Kaden Woodall, Conrad Woodall, and Rebecca Woodall's complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the second and fourth causes of action for fraud. "[E]ven though the plaintiff alleges false representations on the part of the physician or fraudulent concealment, our courts have always treated the action as one for malpractice." (Tell v. Taylor (1961) 191 Cal.App.2d 266, 271.) Based on the conduct alleged in the complaint, this claim is more properly stated as one for medical malpractice because the harm stems from Dr. Stewart's diagnosis and expression of medical opinion. (Compl. 45-48; see Larson v. UHS of Rancho Springs, Inc. (2014) 230 Cal.App.4th 336, 347 ["when a plaintiff asserts a claim against a health care provider on a legal theory other than professional negligence, courts must determine whether the claim is nonetheless based on the health care provider's professional negligence" by examining the legal theory alleged and the provider's alleged conduct] [emphasis in original].) Further, Plaintiffs fail to allege facts to show that Dr. Stewart intended to induce Plaintiffs' reliance on his representations about the cancer in Kaden Woodall's eye. Because Plaintiffs have already pled causes of action for medical malpractice, there would be no reason to allow leave to amend. Hart v. Browne (1980) 103 Cal.App.3d 947 is distinguishable because that case involved a physician who gave allegedly fraudulent advice to attorneys about another doctor's liability for medical malpractice. (Id. at 951.) It could be inferred from medical records and the questions asked by the attorneys that the doctor's statements were false and likely to be relied upon. (Id. at 958.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN SCHMITT VS. RUTH A MAGINNIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Amended Subpoenas To Kasier Hospital - San Francisco And Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2019, Line 5 - DEFENDANT RUTH MAGINNIS' Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Amended Subpoenas To Kasier Hospital - San Francisco And Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. JPT Highman will contact the parties with a substantive tentative ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, then the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing if the tentative ruling is contested. If the tentative is not contested and there is no hearing on the motion, then Defendant must timely comply with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1312. =(302/JPT)
